_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to understand US Code Titles as they apply to targeting in support of operations in cyberspace.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to assess and communicate the security implications of emerging AI technologies, including foundation models, autonomous agents, and AI-generated content, to both technical and executive audiences.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-001 (DG, cosine: 0.402), OP-S-004 (OP, cosine: 0.399).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.5/10 (raw 0.247). Shared vocabulary: [ability]. DCWF-only terms [apply, code, cyberspace, operations, support, targeting] vs WIDAI-only terms [agents, ai, assess, audiences, autonomous, communicate] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.853 (P=0.856, R=0.851). Balanced P/R — the 17-word DCWF element and 26-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Ability to understand US Code Titles as they apply to targeting in support of op" and "Ability to assess and communicate the security implications of emerging AI techn" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.404 > STS 0.247 (gap +0.157). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ability] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.6%. Shared tokens: [ability]. DCWF-only: [apply, code, cyberspace, operations, support, targeting]. WIDAI-only: [agents, ai, assess, audiences, autonomous, communicate].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.157 with low STS 0.247. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
